Title: Internet down
Notes: Customer experienced some outage earlier in the day when their systems rebooted. Everything came back online except internet. They contacted AT&T who told them that they needed to update the IP Address on their firewall. Checked the Unifi portal, but could not communicate with the device because it was offline. Went onsite to physically connect to the Unifi and updated the IP address per AT&T. Rebooted and verified that internet access was restored.

Title: Internet going out intermittently
Notes: Internet connection keeps dropping. Will not stay live for more than a couple of minutes. Check firewall settings and everything seems correct. Restored firewall backup taken a week ago and found that this fixed the issue. Tested for several minutes with continuous ping with no drops. Rob tested for a few minutes and confirmed that it is working much better than earlier.

Title: QNAP firmware 5.2.7.3297 upgrade for TALY
Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the TALY environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalize the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. the firmware upgrade completed successfully.

Title: Internet is not working. AT&T advised they check on router port 4
Notes: Verified that Internet working from AT&T router and problem was on Unifi firewall. Saw that internet connection was plugged into SPF port 9 instead of the "internet" port on the firewall. Moved the cable over and rebooted the firewall. Verified that internet came back online. Jocelyn reported that internet seemed throttled when connected to their main WiFi ssid. Saw that 2.4Ghz was enabled but 5GHz was disabled. Enabled the 5GHz and that doubled their speed when connected from wifi on her mac. Provided recommendation for replacement battery for UPS.

Title: Robert reported that Vickie could not access shares from outside the office
Notes: Investigated issue remotely and found that Vickie's PC could not be reached. Went onsite and found that the IP address that was mapped on Vickie's mac was different from the current public IP. Updated the IP Address on the mac, updated the firewall rule to include both ports since we moved the cable last time, installed MyRemote on Vickie's PC. Fixed shared drive access for Robert and Benjamin as well. Discussed Filemaker issue with Jocelyn and found that the IP she gave me did not match her Mac. Will update that once I get back to the office.

Title: Re: Talley and Associates
Notes:
As per Ravi’s instructions, I tried to call the users for tool and backup setup, but both were not available, so the tasks have been scheduled for Monday morning.
He then instructed me to verify the QNAP storage space and configure Time Machine backup retention to prevent the QNAP from filling up. After researching retention settings, I found that while retention can be managed through macOS, it should ideally be handled via QNAP. We can use a single shared folder for all users, but Ravi requested creating individual users and folders for Time Machine. I attempted this, but QNAP does not provide that option.
After further research, no alternative option was found. I confirmed the available space with him: 4 TB free out of a 7 TB disk.

Title: Re: Talley and Associates
Notes:
I called QNAP support and explained the issue during a remote session. We attempted to set up the configuration, and as per their suggestion, the best way to restrict Time Machine usage for each user is to create individual thick volumes for each user and then set their Time Machine backups into a shared folder within that volume. This approach allows setting a specific size for each user’s backup and prevents exceeding that limit without dealing with global quotas for all users.
Following their recommendation, I created a volume for each username with 750 GB allocated, then created a shared folder within that volume, set it up for Time Machine, and created the user with access to that folder. I then configured the Time Machine backup for Christina-TM and removed her access from the old shared folder for time machine backup.

Title: Re: Talley and Associates

So guided the user to remove the application and reboot the system...user did but still not getting the full acess...

So tried multiple times and got the full access and install ManageEngine MyRemote and Huntress on the system.

However again lost the Access...User said she has some urgent work and need the system..user said to call backa at 3pm...

Called the user at 3Pm again but user said she is still busy on project and aksed for a call back tomorrow at 3pm...

So will call the user tomorrow....

Title: Re: Talley and Associates
Notes: As per the recommendation from support, the best way to restrict Time Machine usage for each user is to create individual thick volumes for each user and then set their Time Machine backups into a shared folder within that volume. This approach allows us to allocate a specific size for each user’s backup and prevents exceeding that limit without relying on global quotas. Following this guidance, I created a separate volume for each username with 750 GB allocated, then created a shared folder within that volume, configured it for Time Machine, and assigned the user access to that folder. I then set up the Time Machine backup for Christina-TM and removed her access from the old shared folder previously used for backups. After completing these steps, I attempted to contact the user to assist with configuring their setup using the new folders and usernames and to confirm the removal of the old Time Machine folder.

Title: Re: Talley and Associates
Notes:
as Robert shared the login details Thank you for sharing the Porkbun login credentials — I’ve logged in and verified the account successfully. I also attempted to add the required DMARC DNS records from Porkbun. During this process, I confirmed that while talleyassoc.com is registered with Porkbun, the domain is not using Porkbun’s nameservers.

Based on a WHOIS and nameserver check, the domain is currently using the following authoritative nameservers:

Ns1 - 4.hostinger.com

Because DNS is hosted at Hostinger, any DNS records (including DMARC) must be added in Hostinger’s DNS zone. Records added in Porkbun will not be published or detected.

Next step:

 Please provide the Hostinger login credentials so we can add the DMARC record directly in Hostinger on your behalf and complete the verification.

 Once access is provided, we’ll take care of the rest. Robert request to send the details to Jocelyn so sent the details to her but didn't get any response.

Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the TALY environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalise the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then re-scanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.
